description: ol-too-many-inputs (issue #111): user procedures are never variadic, so their total-parameter ceiling is a hard maximum. `f` takes two parameters; the parenthesized call `(f 1 2 3)` supplies three, so the static arity check reports too-many with expected = the ceiling and actual = the supplied count. The reader caps a bare call at the procedure's arity (extra tokens become stray statements), so the over-arity case is only reachable through the parenthesized form.
